join_order.notes: Unchanged from iter_0: single-threaded scan of c_mktsegment + c_custkey, build uint8_t cust_ok[1500001]. 1.5 MB, L3-resident.

COMPACT OrderHashMap: OrderSlot is 8 bytes — { int32_t key; int16_t orderdate; int8_t shippriority; int8_t _pad; }. orderdate stored as raw int16_t (max qualifying value = 9203 < 32767, fits). Capacity = 4M slots (next power-of-2 >= 2 * 1.46M = 2.92M). Memory: 4M x 8 = 32 MB. Combined with qualifying_orders bitset (7.5 MB), hot working set = 39.5 MB < LLC (44 MB) — eliminates LLC miss penalty on the 5.9M hash probes. LF ~0.36, expected ~1.3 probes per successful lookup.

filters.notes: lineitem sorted ASC by l_shipdate; ~273 of 600 blocks skipped entirely
Bit-packed bitset prefilter (7.5 MB, L3-resident) rejects ~82% before compact hash map lookup

scan.access_pattern: sequential with zone-map block skipping (morsels = one zone-map block = 100K rows)

data_structures.customer_filter: bitset — uint8_t cust_ok[1500001], zero-initialized, 1.5 MB, L3-resident
data_structures.orders_prefilter: bit-packed bitset — uint64_t qualifying_orders[(60000001+63)/64], 7.5 MB, L3-resident; rejects ~82% of qualifying-shipdate lineitem rows before hash lookup
data_structures.orders_metadata: compact_hash_map — 4M slots x 8 bytes = 32 MB; struct OrderSlot { int32_t key; int16_t orderdate; int8_t shippriority; int8_t _pad; }; sentinel key=-1; Knuth multiplicative hash; linear probing; LF ~0.36; fits fully in 44 MB LLC alongside bitset (39.5 MB total hot set)
data_structures.aggregation: thread_local flat open-addressing AggHashMap — power-of-2 capacity (256K slots); struct AggSlot { int32_t key; int32_t orderdate; int32_t shippriority; double revenue; } = 20 bytes/slot; 256K x 20 = 5 MB/thread; revenue accumulated as double (SSE2, not x87 long double)

phase_breakdown.phase1_customer: single_threaded — 7 MB total I/O (c_mktsegment 1.4 MB + c_custkey 5.7 MB); build cust_ok bitset
phase_breakdown.phase2_orders: parallel scan — each thread scans a row range; collects qualifying OrderRow into thread-local vector; sequential merge builds compact OrderHashMap + qualifying_orders bitset
phase_breakdown.phase3_lineitem: morsel_driven parallel — atomic block index; zone-map block list pre-built; each thread claims blocks from next_block counter; thread-local flat AggHashMap (256K slots); revenue as double
phase_breakdown.phase4_merge: range-partitioned parallel merge — divide orderkey space into nthreads ranges; thread i merges all local AggHashMaps for keys in its range into a thread-local result vector; no shared writes; then single-pass collect and partial_sort top-10

indexes.purpose: skip_blocks — skip lineitem blocks where zones[b].max_date <= 9204; eliminates ~273/600 blocks (~45.5% of lineitem data)
not_used — 240 MB random-access array avoided; in-memory compact hash map + bitset is faster and L3-resident
not_used — direct customer column scan with bitset build is cheaper

aggregation: flat_hash_group_by — thread-local flat open-addressing AggHashMap (256K slots, 5 MB/thread); key=l_orderkey (uniquely determines orderdate+shippriority); revenue as double; parallel range-partitioned merge after scan

output.strategy: partial_sort (nth_element + sort on top-10 candidates from merged result vector)

optimization_notes: Root cause (Category B): The iter_0 OrderHashMap used 8M slots x 12 bytes = 96 MB, far exceeding the 44 MB LLC. The 5.9M qualifying lineitem rows that pass the bitset prefilter must probe this structure with random access, causing an estimated 54% LLC miss rate and significant memory-latency overhead. Fix: compact OrderSlot from 12 to 8 bytes by storing orderdate as raw int16_t (max qualifying value = 9203 < INT16_MAX) and shippriority as int8_t; reduce capacity to 4M slots (LF ~0.36 for 1.46M entries). Result: 4M x 8 = 32 MB hash map. Combined with the 7.5 MB qualifying_orders bitset, the hot random-access working set is 39.5 MB, which fits within the 44 MB LLC, eliminating the cache-miss bottleneck on hash probes. Secondary changes (Category D + C): (1) replace std::unordered_map thread-local aggregation maps with flat open-addressing AggHashMaps (256K slots x 20 bytes = 5 MB/thread) to eliminate pointer-chasing overhead; (2) switch revenue accumulation from long double (x87 80-bit) to double (SSE2 64-bit) for ~2x FP throughput; (3) parallelize the aggregation merge by range-partitioning the orderkey space across threads, so all 64 threads merge concurrently instead of one thread performing ~5.8M serial insertions into a shared global map.
